rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Adoption Of Summary Judgment Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: (part 1 of 2) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner Shanda Love ("Petitioner")'s Motion for Reconsideration of Adoption of Summary Judgment Tentative Ruling (the "Motion"). The Motion is DENIED for the reasons set forth below. Petitioner relies on Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 473(b) to request that the Court reconsider its Order Granting Respondent Shawny Williams Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative Summary Adjudication (the "Summary Judgment Order") on the ground that she failed to contest the Court's tentative ruling on the Motion as a result of her unfamiliarity with the Court's procedure therefor. Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 1008, any party affected by an order may, within ten days after service of a notice of entry of order, make an application to the same judge or court to reconsider the matter based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law. Petitioner does not cite new or different law as her basis for the Motion and attempts only to set forth new or different facts or circumstances. A new or different fact is one that was not available to the party when the underlying motion was presented to the court; it is not enough that the fact is new to the court. (New York Times Co. v. Superior Ct. (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 213). As applied here, Petitioner does not set forth any new facts that would bear on the underlying Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative Summary Adjudication (the "Summary Judgment Motion"). Instead, Petitioner pleads ignorance of judicial processes and seeks to invoke Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 473(b). Although the Court acknowledges Petitioner is a self-represented party, the "alleged ignorance of legal matters or failure to properly represent [one]self can hardly constitute 'mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect' as those terms are used in section 473." (Hopkins & Carley v. Gens (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1401, 1414, citing Goodson v. Bogerts, Inc. (1967) 252 Cal.App.2d 32). As such, Petitioner has failed to set forth new or different facts that warrant reconsideration of the Summary Judgment Order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Reconsideration Of Adoption Of Summary Judgment Tentative Ruling (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: (part 2 of 2) When moving for reconsideration based on a new or different circumstance, the party should explain how the new or different circumstance affects the merits of the matter. (See Scott Co. v. U.S. Fid. & Guar. Ins. (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 197, 206 n.8, disapproved on other grounds in Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1094; see also Johnston v. Corrigan (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 553, 556, holding that the trial court's failure to consider a timely filed memorandum of points and authorities was a sufficient showing of new circumstances.). Here, Petitioner requests that the court reconsider its Summary Judgment Order "so that the [C]ourt may take into consideration Petitioner[']s opposition" to certain statutes cited in Respondent's Summary Judgment Motion (Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Motion for Reconsideration of Adoption of Summary Judgment Tentative Ruling, at p. 1:25). However, as reflected in the Summary Judgment Order, the Court considered Petitioner's untimely filed opposition to the Summary Judgment Motion in deciding the matter. (Summary Judgment Order at p. 2:4). Moreover, Petitioner repeats the substance of her opposition to the Summary Judgment Motion here in the instant motion. As such, Petitioner has failed to present new or different circumstances to warrant reconsideration of the Summary Judgment Order because the Court already considered Petitioner's untimely opposition to the Summary Judgment Motion when it issued the Summary Judgment Order. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
